title: Comparing to GANs' Single Forward Pass
concept: A GAN generates an image in one network call; a diffusion model may need 1000 network calls per image, a massive speed difference.

exercises.prompt: If a robot could draw your picture in 1 snap OR in 1000 tiny careful steps, which one sounds faster?

options.text: They are exactly the same speed

exercises.explain: One snap is way faster than doing something 1000 times! That's the big speed difference we'll learn about today.

cards.title: One Click vs. A Thousand Clicks!
cards.body: Imagine two artists. One draws your portrait in a single flash of magic. The other redraws it 1000 times, erasing and fixing tiny bits each time until it looks perfect. Both can make great art... but one is A LOT slower!

cards.title: Forward Pass Speed
cards.body: A GAN makes an image using just ONE trip through its network — like flipping one switch. A diffusion model makes an image by cleaning up noise little by little, often calling its network hundreds or even 1000 times for ONE picture.

cards.title: Instant Noodles vs. Slow-Cooked Stew
cards.body: A GAN is like instant noodles — pop it in the microwave once and it's ready! A diffusion model is like a slow-cooked stew — you stir it a little bit, again and again, hundreds of times, until it becomes delicious. Both taste good, but one takes way longer to make.

cards.title: Making One Cat Picture
cards.body:
GAN: Feed in random noise → ONE pass through the generator → done! A cat appears instantly.

Diffusion model: Start with pure noise → run the denoising network → repeat this up to 1000 times, slowly removing noise → a cat picture appears at the very end.

cards.title: Myth: Slower Always Means Worse
cards.body: You might think doing something 1000 times is a waste of time. But it's not a flaw — diffusion models take those extra steps ON PURPOSE, because it helps them create super detailed, high-quality images. Slow isn't always bad, and fast isn't always better!

cards.title: Speed Showdown
cards.body: Compare how many network calls each model needs to create ONE image.

right.text: Up to 1000 passes = slow, careful image

exercises.prompt: Which model typically needs only ONE forward pass to create an image?

options.text: Both need exactly the same number of passes

exercises.explain: A GAN's generator only needs to run once through its network to produce a full image — that's its superpower: speed!

exercises.prompt: True or False: A diffusion model can take up to 1000 network calls to make just one image.
exercises.explain: True! Diffusion models slowly remove noise step by step, and that can take hundreds or even 1000 steps to finish one image.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the sentence.
exercises.sentence: A GAN creates an image in one ___, while a diffusion model may need 1000.
exercises.explain: A 'forward pass' means one trip through the network. GANs need just one; diffusion models need many.

exercises.prompt: Which of these is the BEST reason diffusion models are slower than GANs at generating images?

options.text: Diffusion models need many steps to slowly remove noise, while GANs finish in one step
GANs don't use any computer code

exercises.explain: The real reason is the number of steps: diffusion models repeat their denoising process many times, while GANs do it all in a single pass.

exercises.prompt: Your friend says, 'GANs and diffusion models take the exact same amount of time to make one image.' Are they right?

options.text: No — GANs use one forward pass, while diffusion models often use hundreds or 1000 passes, making GANs much faster per image
Yes — both always take exactly one forward pass
Yes — both always take exactly 1000 forward passes
No — diffusion models are always faster than GANs

exercises.explain: GANs generate an image in a single forward pass, making them very fast. Diffusion models repeat their denoising step many times (sometimes up to 1000), which makes them much slower per image — even though the results can be very high quality.
